name: Middle Chinese
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY NODE (not a modelled G2P target). Middle Chinese (c. 600 CE, Qieyun) is the ancestor of the modern Sinitic varieties and the SOURCE of the Sino-Xenic literary borrowings in Japanese (kango), Korean (hanja) and Vietnamese (Sino-Vietnamese). It exists here only so the ancestry / phylogenetic-distance metrics of the modern varieties that reference it have a node to resolve to; NO phonology is claimed or reconstructed and the minimal grapheme set is a placeholder (afa.json / trk.json stub pattern).

sources.author: Pulleyblank, E. G.
sources.title: Middle Chinese: A Study in Historical Phonology
sources.notes: Reference for Middle Chinese and Sino-Xenic strata (ancestry node only).
